cash_trend.note: Prior month comparison uses 4/24 file (21 days back) — closest available snapshot. True prior-month comp will be available next week.

summary.note: Filtered to Listed and Make Ready only. 12 homes still need projection data.

cfo_summary: Total available cash sits at $214,510 across all entities. After this week's approved payments of $11,022, projected end-of-week balance is $203,488. The $6,000 Sunset Plumbing invoice is on hold pending dispute resolution per the 5/13 L10 decision.

Cash is up $51,425 from last week (31.5%) and up $44,947 from same period prior month (26.5%). The reversal of last week's downward trend is driven by sale proceeds from the FS2 closings that cleared this period and the DA:43 cash deal at $36K. The trend now sits in healthy territory.

Country Aire remains thin at $2,594 available after the FS to CA $5K transfer from last week. No payments approved against CA this week so it stays flat. The structural issue is still the Chase utility billing artifact catching two months of charges. Once that normalizes next billing cycle, CA should rebuild on its own from lot rent collections.

GRP carries the working capital load again this week with $7,188 in approved maintenance and contractor payments against $21,919 available. After payments GRP projects to $14,731. The two largest single items are the Central Air condenser replacement on WW:6D at $2,300 and the C&K Construction seasonal preventive maintenance sweep at roughly $2,500 across multiple homes.

The watch list still carries $170,000 in other obligations with zero reserved. $97,000 for Tim and Trent distributions, $38,000 for FS flood plane removal, and $35,000 for the 2031 W 3rd acquisition. Trent meets Tim Tuesday on rent credit strategy; the distribution conversation may move. Home obligations show $217K in liens across 13 active homes, but only 1 home has a projected sale price entered. Until Trent fills in projections, the net reserve number is not reliable.

for_ownership.label: Projected end-of-week cash
for_ownership.paragraph: We close this week at roughly $203K available across all accounts after $11K in maintenance and contractor bills go out. The big movement is sale proceeds clearing from the FS2 home closings and the DA:43 cash deal, which together bring us back above where we started the month. The $6K Sunset Plumbing bill is on hold while the team disputes the amount. Country Aire is still tight from a utility billing artifact but the other entities are in good shape. The biggest forward items remain the Tim and Trent distributions, the flood plane removal at Four Seasons, and the 2033 West 3rd home purchase from Juan once title arrives.

next_month_preview: June payroll runs the second week and lands roughly $26K across entities. GRP and GG should be in position to cover their share.

Remaining FS2 lien releases on closed sales will continue clearing as title work catches up. The next likely candidates are FS2:01, FS2:029, FS2:047, totaling roughly $56K. WW:6D ($7.8K) is gated on Hambo finishing the rehab.

If Trent and Tim move forward with rent credit conversions on FS1-77 and FS1-78, those shift from sale-only to occupied rentals over the next 60 days. Cash flow impact is positive but valuation effect is muted (lot rent only matters for refi appraisals).
2033 West 3rd home purchase from Juan Cardoza expected to land in June or early July at $35K once title arrives. Sell as-is strategy, target buyer in pipeline before payment to Juan.
The FS1 + FS2 flood plane removal ($38K combined) remains unfunded. Need a funding source decision before the contractor is engaged. BvA was suppressed this week because no P&L was uploaded.
recap_notes: Sunset Plumbing $6K invoice held pending dispute resolution per 5/13 L10 decision.
Cash is up $51K from last week, driven by FS2 sale closings and DA:43 cash deal at $36K.
Country Aire still tight at $2,594. Last week's $5K FS to CA transfer cleared. Watch through next utility billing cycle.
C&K Construction seasonal preventive maintenance sweep completed across 14 homes for roughly $2,500 total.
WW:6D condenser replacement done at $2,300. Home is in final checklist before listing.
DA:43 confirmed sold and cash deposit cleared. Remove from Home Obligations tracker.
CP:58Powerline showing as Make Ready in tracker but 5/11 Ops Review approved listing at $30K sale only. Tracker needs status update.
Robert's Electric and Juan Cardoza vendor payments flagged urgent on 5/11 — confirm with Trent that these cleared.
Home tracker still missing projected sale prices on 12 of 13 active homes. Net reserve math depends on these.
No P&L uploaded for May 5/15. BvA and Monthly Outlook suppressed.
